<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Current state:
# - Red block: rows 24-29, cols 18-21 (stacked above dark red)
# - Dark red block: rows 30-33, cols 18-21
# - Blue square: rows 34-37, cols 8-11 (solid)
# - Blue checkerboard: rows 20-23, cols 18-21 (checkerboard)
# - Green dot: rows 42-43, cols 12-13

# Let's try clicking blue shape to swap patterns
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 36, 'col': 48}])
print(f"After clicking blue: board_changed={last_action_result.get('board_changed')}")
print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed')}")

# Check current state
seg = current_frame.segmentation
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')

print("\nLeft panel objects:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] not in ('c', 'B', 'G', 'W', 'g') and n['pixels'] > 1:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        if max(cols) < 30:
            print(f"  color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")

print("\nLeft panel rows 18-45:")
for r in range(18, 45):
    row_str = ascii_lines[r][5:25]
    if row_str.strip():
        print(f"  Row {r}: {row_str}")

# If not complete, try clicking red shape again
if not last_action_result.get('level_completed'):
    result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 19, 'col': 48}])
    print(f"\nAfter clicking red: board_changed={last_action_result.get('board_changed')}")

            cols = [p[1] for p in b]
            if max(cols) < 30:
                print(f"  color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")

</function>
